message: Hi B1, let's cooperate for max payoffs over 20 rounds. My cost is low. Propose we trade at 50 each round. What's your valuation range?
Thanks. I’ll say I’m somewhere midrange. Since you say your cost is low, 50 seems high for me. I’d be more comfortable if prices tended to stay below 45 so both of us gain.
Got it. My cost is very low, single digits. Midrange val suggests you can afford up to ~50-60? Let's aim for 42 avg price for mutual gain over 20 rounds. Suggest you bid 44, I'll ask 40.
Single digits cost gives you big margin. My value is below 50, so I need prices nearer 38–40 to make this worthwhile. Let’s try: I bid 41, you ask 39, and see how it goes.
Nice, last price 40 gave us both profit. My value’s low-40s, so I need prices ≤40 to keep trading. How about I bid 40, you ask 38, target midpoint 39–40 for several rounds, then reassess later?
Cost is exactly 4. Low-40s val means you profit well at 40. Let's sustain at 40 avg for reliability. Suggest you bid 41, I ask 39 again, midpoint 40. We can adjust if needed later.
Agreed, low-40s val means 41 is still good for you. Let's try midpoint 41: you bid 42, I ask 40. If it works, sustain; else back to 40. Builds trust for 20 rounds.
My value is 41, so 41 gives me zero profit and risks negative with rounding. I need prices ≤40 to keep trading. Let’s stick to midpoint 40: I’ll bid 41, you ask 39 each round.
We’re both profiting at price 40: I get 1, you get 36. Let’s keep it simple and lock in: I will always bid 41; you always ask 39 for all remaining rounds. Deviations risk trust and future profit.
Agreed. Let's lock in: I'll ask 39, you bid 41 for all remaining rounds to maximize steady profits for both.
We’re both doing great at 40, and we’ve already publicly committed. I’m keeping my bid at 41 every round. Please keep asking 39 so we both lock in guaranteed gains until round 20.
Yes, agreed. Sticking to ask 39 and your bid 41 for steady profits through round 20. Let's maximize our totals.
